idea.title: Shadow Cast
idea.hook: Position a handful of blocks between a lamp and the back wall so the shadow they cast matches today's target silhouette exactly.
idea.prototype: A point light at left, a back wall at right, and a budget of opaque blocks you drag onto vertical rails between them. Each block projects a scaled shadow onto the wall; the union of shadows is compared to a target dark-region silhouette. Win: overlap >= tolerance with no excess. Lose: all blocks placed and the shadow mismatches. Per-level target silhouette. Controls: drag blocks along rails / tap to resize.
idea.mutation_of: crazygames Maze: Path of Light + Fluid Enigma (light/optics) + reddit silhouette-guess games
idea.why_novel: shadow PROJECTION — reasoning backwards from a desired 2D shadow to block placement/scale; language-free, infinite content
idea.lose_condition: Final block placed (blocksLeft === 0) and committed shadow overlap < WIN_OVERLAP (86%) or excess > MAX_EXCESS (22%) -> gs = 'GAMEOVER'.

sources.ref: daily-mix 2026-05-24 (transfer_bet / vehicle_crash archetype slot; mechanic reframed to a light/shadow puzzle)

synthesizer_brief: Daily MOBILE-TRANSFER slot assigned the vehicle_crash CTR archetype, but the mechanic brief was a shadow-projection puzzle. Built the puzzle (not a racing game): a point light, a back wall, and a budget of draggable/resizable opaque blocks on rails. Each block casts a scale-by-distance shadow (scale = wallDist / lightToBlockDist); the union of shadow rects is compared to a per-level target silhouette on a 16x24 grid. Win = match >= 86% AND spill <= 22%. Lose = run out of blocks while mismatched. CTR art borrows the archetype's high-contrast saturated-foreground punch (warm light beam vs deep shadow, bold cube cluster) WITHOUT any vehicle.
